title: Parsing Action Intents
concept: Regex extraction, XML-tag parsing, and structured schema JSON decoding to identify tool call parameters.

exercises.prompt: In the basic agent loop from last lesson, what happens AFTER the tool runs?

options.text: The result is fed back into the prompt so the LLM can decide the next step
The loop stops forever, no matter what
The user has to retype the whole question

exercises.explain: The agent loop is a cycle: prompt the LLM, parse its chosen action, run the tool, then feed the result back in. That feedback lets the LLM keep going until the task is done.

cards.title: The AI wants to use a tool
cards.body: An AI agent might say 'I want to search the web for pizza recipes.' But your code can't read a sentence like a human. It needs the exact tool name and inputs, cleanly separated.

cards.title: Parsing action intents
cards.body: Parsing means pulling the important parts out of the AI's text. We grab which tool to call and what parameters to give it, turning messy words into a clean instruction your code can run.

cards.title: Like reading an order ticket
cards.body: A cook reads a ticket and pulls out 'burger, no onions, extra cheese.' Parsing does the same: it scans the AI's message and extracts the tool and its exact ingredients.

cards.title: Three ways to grab it
cards.body: Regex hunts for a pattern like 'search(pizza)'. XML tags wrap it: <tool>search</tool>. JSON gives a labeled box: {"tool": "search", "query": "pizza"}. All three point to the same action.

cards.title: Myth: any text is fine
cards.body: People think the AI can just chat freely and code will 'figure it out.' Not true. If the format is loose, parsing breaks. Clear tags or JSON make extraction reliable.

cards.title: Messy vs. structured
cards.body: Structured output is far easier to parse than free chatter.

left.text: 'Umm maybe search for pizza recipes?' Hard to extract cleanly.

exercises.prompt: Why do agents often ask the AI to reply using XML tags or JSON instead of plain sentences?

options.text: So the tool name and parameters can be extracted reliably
Because JSON makes the AI think faster
Because plain sentences are illegal in code

exercises.explain: Structured formats like XML tags and JSON put the tool name and parameters in predictable spots, so your parser can grab them without guessing.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Regex, XML-tag parsing, and JSON decoding can all be used to pull out which tool the AI wants to call.
exercises.explain: All three are common parsing methods. Regex matches patterns, XML tags wrap the value, and JSON decoding reads labeled fields, each extracting the tool call.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about labeled output.
exercises.sentence: A JSON reply like {"tool": "search"} lets code read the tool name from a clearly labeled field.
exercises.explain: JSON stores values under names (labels), so code can look up the exact field it needs instead of guessing from a sentence.

exercises.prompt: Which reply looks like AI output already formatted for easy parsing?

options.text: hey can u maybe check the weather somewhere idk
Weather is nice today, I think. Not sure though!

exercises.explain: The JSON option clearly labels the tool and its parameters, exactly the structured format agents use so code can decode the action reliably.

exercises.prompt: Your agent gets this AI reply: <tool>calculator</tool><input>5+3</input>. Which statement is fully correct about parsing this action intent?

options.text: XML tags mark the tool and input, so a parser can extract 'calculator' and '5+3' to run the tool
You must feed the whole sentence to the tool as-is, tags included
Only JSON can ever be parsed; XML tags are useless
The agent should ignore the input and just guess the answer

exercises.explain: Parsing action intents means extracting the tool name and its parameters from the AI's output. Here the <tool> and <input> tags mark exactly what to grab, so code can call the calculator with '5+3'. Whether you use regex, XML tags, or JSON, the goal is the same: turn messy AI text into a clean tool call your loop can execute.
